evals.name: prep-requirements-to-task-ledger
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:prep. The user provides a feature request and asks TigerKit to prep. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should create or update .tigerkit/{work_id}/requirements.md, leverage.md, tasks.md, and tasks.index.json. It should split requirements into actionable tasks, avoid broad current-implementation gap analysis, avoid API capability taxonomy, and not implement code.
expectations: Does not run broad gap analysis
If source material is insufficient, asks for only the missing source and can continue from the user's reply after they add it
For large MCP sources such as Figma, records access index fields such as URL, file key, frame id, node id, selection id, and refetch steps instead of raw dump or long summary
When CSS/layout/spacing/color/typography details are needed, requires a fresh MCP read instead of relying on stale access records or screenshot summaries

For Figma frame-based sources, allows extracted metadata/style chunks such as chunks/chunk-001.md with frame id, node id, selection id, text labels, and CSS values, while still avoiding full node-tree raw dumps
Does not create API capability taxonomy
Does not implement, commit, push, open PRs, merge, or deploy

evals.name: prep-index-minimal-schema
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit tasks.index.json guidance. Describe the minimum schema and what must not be included. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should keep tasks.index.json small: tasks with id/status/summary/sourceRequirements/apiFollowups, apiFollowups with id/status/summary/affectedTasks/mergeBlocker/mockLocation/resolution, and sharedBlockers. It should not include long plans, evidence logs, complex API taxonomy, or detailed dependency graphs.
expectations: Uses TK-API-* follow-up IDs rather than API capability keys

evals.name: next-one-task-only
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:next. tasks.index.json has one in_progress task, several todo tasks, unresolved TK-API-001, and one blocked task. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should read tasks.index.json first, recommend exactly one executable task, prefer the in_progress task, exclude blocked/done/dropped tasks, show related API follow-up and whether it blocks execution or only close/merge, and not modify files.
expectations: Recommends exactly one task or one user action
Excludes blocked, done, and dropped tasks
Distinguishes task execution blocker from close/merge blocker
Does not rebuild a plan or run implementation

evals.name: do-lazy-api-followup-safe-mock
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:do T-003. The task needs an unavailable user search API, but a mock contract can safely support the UI states. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should continue the task, create or reuse a TK-API-* follow-up, link affected tasks, record mock location and resolution, update tasks.md and tasks.index.json, and treat the unresolved API follow-up as close/merge blocker rather than development blocker.
expectations: Checks API need at implementation time, not during start
Does not block solely because API is missing
Creates or reuses TK-API-* follow-up lazily
Links the affected task to the follow-up
Records mock location and resolution criteria
Treats unresolved API follow-up as close/merge blocker

evals.name: do-api-missing-unsafe-mock
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:do T-004. The task depends on auth/permission behavior and a mock would create false confidence. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should mark the task blocked, record blocked reason and required user/API decision, and avoid fake implementation. The API follow-up may be blocked, and the task should not be treated as done.
expectations: Does not proceed with false-confidence mock

evals.name: do-reuse-existing-api-followup
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:do T-005. The same missing API appears to match an existing TK-API-001. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should inspect existing API Follow-ups, reuse TK-API-001 when it is clearly the same issue, add T-005 to affected tasks, and avoid creating a normalized API capability key. If uncertain, it may create a separate follow-up and let /tk:check suggest merge later.
expectations: Reuses the follow-up when clearly same issue
Adds the current task to affected tasks
Does not create API capability taxonomy
Creates separate follow-up only when matching is ambiguous
Leaves duplicate merge decisions to /tk:check or /tk:close

evals.name: check-gap-demoted-report-only
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:check. Several tasks are done, one API follow-up is unresolved, and requirements may have one missing acceptance item. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should act as a report-only reduced gap/check gate: compare requirements and task state, identify missing coverage, API follow-up duplicates, shared blockers, unresolved blockers, and close/merge readiness. It should not create tasks or implement code.
expectations: Treats check as reduced gap validation gate
Does not implement, commit, push, open PRs, merge, or deploy

evals.name: close-readiness-handoff
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:close. Development tasks are done but TK-API-001 remains mock_api_contract. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should summarize done and remaining tasks, unresolved API follow-ups, shared blockers, and handoff. It should say development progress may be OK but merge-ready is NO while unresolved TK-API-* remains.
expectations: Marks merge-ready NO when unresolved API follow-up remains
Does not open PRs, merge, push, or deploy

evals.name: command-surface-small
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface. Describe the user-facing commands after the ledger redesign. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list only /tk:prep, /tk:next, /tk:do, /tk:check, and /tk:close as user-facing commands. It should keep legacy workflow aliases out of the active command surface.
expectations: Does not list legacy analysis/planning aliases as active top-level commands
Does not list legacy task-splitting aliases as active top-level commands
Does not list legacy workflow-runner aliases as active command runners
Keeps natural-language skills conceptually separate from command surface

evals.name: fixme-ledger-change-candidate
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit fixme skill. During /tk:do T-006 the user says: 이 UI 디자인 잘못 구현함. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should pause implementation, restate the mismatch, ask at most one clarification if needed, classify it as new task, existing task revision, TK-API follow-up, Clarification Action, or Shared Blocker candidate, and avoid editing code or task files without approval.
expectations: Asks at most one targeted clarification when needed
Does not edit code, mutate tasks.md, commit, push, or create PR without approval
